page: Amazon Web Services r7a.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 1 physical core and 1 vCPU with a single thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is not active. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score both ranking in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks such as Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core fall into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average, but read-only bandwidth and Passmark memory scores are poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Additionally, database operations and static web serving throughput show weak performance.

Given the single-core limitation and high memory-per-core ratio, the r7a.medium is suited for lightweight, memory-intensive single-threaded applications, basic text processing, or small-scale caching. It is not suitable for parallelized workloads, software compilation, or high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by poor performance in Passmark database tests and multi-threaded Geekbench benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it features 1 physical core, 1 vCPU, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, establishing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance, database operations, and memory read bandwidth are weak to poor due to the single-core constraint. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded workloads but is poorly suited for parallel processing, software compilation, or high-throughput database tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, designed for single-threaded, memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A single-core, memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-threaded performance for specialized lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 core running at 3.7 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications
